headlines.title: Marco Rubio says 'we're not asking for China's help' with Iran
headlines.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio tells NBC News' Tom Llamas that President Trump brought up Iran during the China summit and made the U.S. position clear. NBC News correspondents Gabe Gutierrez, Courtney Kube and Andrea Mitchell join Meet the Press NOW discuss Trump's bilateral meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping. Bonny Lin, director of the China Power Project at the Center for Strategic and International Studies, weighs in on the Taiwan issue hanging over the summit.

headlines.title: China warns Taiwan tensions could jeopardize U.S. relationship
headlines.description: President Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping visited the Temple of Heaven after their two-hour bilateral meeting. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports that, according to Chinese state media, Taiwan was among the most important topics discussed.

headlines.title: Young Chinese facing ‘life pressure’ resist government push to boost marriage rate
headlines.description: The streets around Beijing’s iconic Forbidden City are often filled with brides and grooms, posing for wedding photos against auspicious red backdrops in a place that strongly reflects Chinese culture and tradition.

headlines.title: Sources: Pentagon has no new munitions contracts amid concerns over weapons shortage
headlines.description: As the war with Iran pushes its munitions stocks to dangerously low levels, the Defense Department has not signed new contracts to replenish its supplies, according to two U.S. officials and two people familiar with the matter. NBC News' Gordon Lubold details the kinds of weapons and their importance to the Middle East conflict.

headlines.title: How Alex Murdaugh Reacted to Overturning of Murder Convictions
headlines.description: In a TODAY exclusive, Alex Murdaugh’s attorneys Dick Harpootlian and Jim Griffin, join TODAY to discuss the South Carolina Supreme Court’s ruling on Wednesday to overturn the double murder convictions and how Murdaugh reacted. "He was very gracious, thankful and he said 'Jim, I'm seeing it, I'm reading it, but it's still hard to believe,'" Griffin says of his conversation with Murduagh. The lawyers also talk about what they expect to be different in the next trial, comment on whether or no

headlines.title: Republicans grapple with how much to have Trump on the campaign trail for the midterms
headlines.description: Trump’s approval rating is sinking amid high prices and the war with Iran, but he’s still the best at turning out the base voters Republicans need for the midterms.

headlines.title: The toxic aftermath of the L.A.-area fires
headlines.description: Since the L.A.-area fires, Altadena residents have found toxic compounds such as lead and asbestos in their living spaces and lots. Many have not returned home.

headlines.title: Why the prices of World Cup resale tickets are starting to drop
headlines.description: After an initial furor over jaw-dropping ticket prices, global soccer fans may be starting to see some relief — at least in the resale market.

headlines.title: Senior minister quits, setting up possible contest to oust British PM
headlines.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has the challenge he dared his mutinous party to produce, setting up a potential clash over the future of the United Kingdom and its ruling center-left Labour Party.

headlines.title: The evolution of album rollouts
headlines.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

headlines.title: Trump presents a midterm dilemma for Republicans: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: In today’s edition, Jonathan Allen explores the delicate balance Republicans are attempting to strike for the midterms

headlines.title: Racial backlash erupts online after California mayor admits to being an agent for China
headlines.description: The resignation of a California mayor who pleaded guilty to acting as a foreign agent for China has sparked backlash and reignited fears of anti-Asian discrimination.
